title: Public HTTP deployment can enable credential and session interception
attacker_controlled_source: A network attacker able to observe or modify traffic to the configured public HTTP panel; deployment configuration selects ADMIN_PUBLIC_BASE_URL and may omit ALLOW_INSECURE_HTTP_AUTH.
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: resolve_http_auth_setting defaults ALLOW_INSECURE_HTTP_AUTH to true whenever ADMIN_PUBLIC_BASE_URL is not HTTPS; auth_transport_is_allowed then permits login over HTTP, while access and refresh cookies derive Secure from the request and can be sent without Secure.
impact: Credentials and bearer session cookies can be captured or altered in transit, enabling administrator/player account takeover and subsequent privileged panel, RCON, database, deployment, or personal-data access.
why_plausible: The behavior is explicit in tracked code and is reachable in supported HTTP deployments. A concrete in-scope deployment .env uses an http public URL without an explicit secure-auth override (the file is ignored and its secrets are not reproduced here).
closest_apparent_control: require_secure_auth_transport and cookie_secure_for_request are intended transport controls, but the scheme-based default and non-Secure cookie behavior make them permissive for HTTP.
validation_recommended: Run the deployed panel through an untrusted HTTP network, verify login/refresh succeeds and Set-Cookie lacks Secure, then confirm a passive/intercepting network observer can replay or modify the captured credentials/token. Verify whether production terminates TLS before FastAPI and whether an explicit false override is enforced.
evidence: admin-web/backend/main.py:281-293 derives a true default for any non-HTTPS public URL
admin-web/backend/main.py:299 wires the derived value into ALLOW_INSECURE_HTTP_AUTH
admin-web/backend/main.py:1462-1473 allows authentication when the flag is true
admin-web/backend/main.py:1512-1518 and 1524-1531 set cookie Secure from request transport
